revision.text:

| spouse = {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|qq=}}<ref> {{cite book | last=Chamberlain | first=B.H. | title=Kojiki: Records of Ancient Matters | publisher=Tuttle Publishing | series=Tuttle Classics | year=2012 | isbn=978-1-4629-0511-9 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=jf_PAgAAQBAJ | access-date=2020-11-21 }} </ref><ref name="fountainhead-1"/>{{rp|278}}

| name = Fuhano Mojikunusunu no Kami
| generation = [[Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|Seventeenth Generation Gods]], Second Generation
| predecessor = {{ill|Yashimajinumi|en|Yashimajinumi|qid=Q55522907|lt=Yashimajinumi|1=Yashimajinumi}}
| successor = {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|qq=}}

| full_name = Fuhano Mojikunusunu no Kami (布波能母遅久奴須奴神)
| other_names = Fuhano Mochiunasunu, Fuhano Mochiunasu
| aliases = 
| divine_Rank = 
| attributes = National Land God

}}
{{ill|Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|ja|布波能母遅久奴須奴神|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|1=Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha-no-Mojikunusunu|qq=}} (布波能母遅久奴須奴神 – Budding Noble Land Dwelling Master)<ref>{{Cite web |title=Fuwanomojikunusunu • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/fuwanomojikunusunu/ |access-date=2023-11-17 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}}</ref> was a Japanese deity associated with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and the {{ill|Izumo Province|en|Izumo Province|qid=Q582846|lt=Izumo Province|1=Izumo Province}}..<ref name="fountainhead-local">{{cite book |last=Herbert |first=J. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402 |title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan |publisher=Taylor & Francis |year=2010 |isbn=978-1-136-90376-2 |series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan |page=402 |access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}} He is the son of {{ill|Yashimajinumi|en|Yashimajinumi|qid=Q55522907|lt=Yashimajinumi|1=Yashimajinumi}}<ref name="fountainhead-1">{{cite book | last=Herbert | first=J. | title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan | publisher=Taylor & Francis | series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan | year=2010 | isbn=978-1-136-90376-2 | url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402 | access-date=2020-11-21 | page=402}}</ref>{{rp|278}}{{rp|278}}

He is one of the [[Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities]]

==Overview==
'''Fuhano Mojikunusunu no Kami''' (布波能母遅久奴須奴神, also spelled Fuhano Mochiunasu or Fuhano Mochiunasunu) is a deity appearing in [[Japanese mythology]] and one of the {{ill|Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|lt=Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|lt=Seventeenth Generation Gods|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventeenth Generation Gods|qq=}} classified as a {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}.

== Overview ==
Fuhano Mojikunusunu no Kami is introduced in the *{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}* as one of the {{ill|Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|lt=Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|lt=Seventeenth Generation Gods|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventeenth Generation Gods|qq=}}. The deity is also mentioned in the {{ill|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|qq=}} under the name *Fuhano Mochiunasu* (布波能母知汙那須奴).

== Interpretations ==
The name of the deity has been analyzed in various ways:
- The component "母遅" (*mochi*) is interpreted as signifying "nobility" or "honor," similar to its usage in {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi|ja|大穴牟遅神|1=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (*Ōnamuchi*) and {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Ōhirume|ja|大日孁貴神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} (*Ōhirume*). "久奴" (*kunu*) is thought to mean "master of the land," suggesting this deity represents an early indication of the eventual appearance of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}.<ref name="#1">Kokugakuin University Kojiki Research Center, "Fuhano Mojikunusunu no Kami."</ref>

- "布波" (*fuha*) derives from the root of the verb "含む" (*fufumu*), meaning "to contain" or "to hold." This evokes the imagery of a "bud" yet to bloom. Combined with "母遅," the name may signify "the noble bud of a promising land."<ref>*Shinchosha Nihon Kokugo Taikei: Kojiki.*</ref>

- Alternatively, "久奴須" is interpreted as "national land" or "soil," symbolizing primordial earth or its formative elements. Under this view, the deity represents the material foundation of the world, linked to {{ill|Konohanachiru-hime|en|Konohanachiru-hime|qid=Q48745675|lt=Konohanachiru-hime|1=Konohanachiru-hime}} through the imagery of delicate cherry blossoms and fertile soil.<ref name="#1"/>

== Genealogy ==
{{ill|Yashimajinumi|en|Yashimajinumi|qid=Q55522907|lt=Yashimajinumi|1=Yashimajinumi}} and {{ill|Konohanachiru-hime|en|Konohanachiru-hime|qid=Q48745675|lt=Konohanachiru-hime|1=Konohanachiru-hime}} are the parents of Fuhano Mojikunusunu no Kami. The deity marries {{ill|Hikawahime|ja|日河比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikawahime|qq=}}, and their child is {{ill|Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|ja|深淵之水夜礼花神|1=Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukabuchi-no-Mizuyarehana|qq=}}.

In the {{ill|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awaga Daimyōjin Genki|qq=}}, it is mentioned that the deity also marries {{ill|Ame-no-Tsudoechine|ja|天之都度閇知泥神|1=Ame-no-Tsudoechine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tsudoechine|qq=}} and has a child named {{ill|Omizunu|en|Omizunu|qid=Q48745416|lt=Omizunu|1=Omizunu}}.{{Eight generations of Izumo}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}


== See Also ==
